content.text: I'll help you with that. Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current state of the screen.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
I see a permission dialog asking to allow bash to access the screen. Let me click "Allow" first.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
Good, the dialog is dismissed. Now let me open Safari and navigate to cmux.dev. I'll click the Safari icon in the dock.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
That opened Finder instead. Let me click on the Safari icon in the dock - it's the compass icon.
Let me close this Finder window and open Safari properly. I'll use the keyboard shortcut to open Safari via Spotlight.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
Spotlight is open. Let me type "Safari" to find and launch it.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
Safari is open. Now let me click on the address bar and navigate to cmux.dev.
Let me wait a moment for the page to load, then take a screenshot.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}

cmux.dev has loaded in Safari showing the cmux website - "The terminal built for coding agents." The page is currently displaying in what appears to be a dark theme. This is the **light mode screenshot** (the site itself has a dark design).

Now let me switch macOS to dark mode. I'll open System Settings via Spotlight.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
System Settings is open. I can see "Appearance" in the left sidebar. Let me click on it to access the dark mode setting.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
I can see the Appearance settings. Currently "Light" mode is selected. I need to click on "Dark" to switch to dark mode.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}

macOS has switched to Dark Mode. I can see the entire System Settings UI is now dark, and the "Dark" option is selected. The tooltip confirms "Use a dark appearance for buttons, menus, and windows."

Now let me switch back to Safari to see cmux.dev in dark mode. I'll click on the Safari window visible behind System Settings.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
System Settings is still in front. Let me click on the Safari window in the dock to bring it to the front.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
That brought Finder to the front again. I can see the Safari window behind with cmux.dev. Let me click directly on the Safari window's title bar area to bring it forward.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
System Settings came to the front instead. Let me close the Finder window and System Settings, then bring Safari to front. I'll use Cmd+Tab to switch to Safari.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}
I can see Safari behind the Finder window with cmux.dev content visible at the bottom. Let me close the Finder window first, then bring Safari fully to the front.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}

Now I can see cmux.dev in Safari with macOS in Dark Mode. The site is fully visible with the dark theme. I notice the heading says "The terminal built for Gem" (likely an animation cycling through terms). The Safari chrome (title bar, toolbar) is now dark, confirming macOS dark mode is active.

Let me take a clean screenshot by clicking on the Safari window to make sure it's the active app.
{"image_width":1024,"image_height":768}

Here's the completed task:

**Screenshot 1 (Light Mode)** - cmux.dev was loaded in Safari while macOS was in Light mode. The Safari chrome had a light/gray appearance.

**Screenshot 2 (Dark Mode)** - After switching macOS to Dark Mode via System Settings > Appearance > Dark, cmux.dev is now shown with Safari's dark chrome (dark title bar and toolbar). The cmux.dev website itself uses a dark design in both modes, but you can see the difference in the Safari UI elements — the title bar, toolbar, and menu bar are now dark-themed.

The site displays **cmux** — "The terminal built for coding agents" — a native macOS app built on Ghostty with features like vertical tabs, notification rings, in-app browser, split panes, and a scriptable API.

result:
Here's the completed task:

**Screenshot 1 (Light Mode)** - cmux.dev was loaded in Safari while macOS was in Light mode. The Safari chrome had a light/gray appearance.

**Screenshot 2 (Dark Mode)** - After switching macOS to Dark Mode via System Settings > Appearance > Dark, cmux.dev is now shown with Safari's dark chrome (dark title bar and toolbar). The cmux.dev website itself uses a dark design in both modes, but you can see the difference in the Safari UI elements — the title bar, toolbar, and menu bar are now dark-themed.

The site displays **cmux** — "The terminal built for coding agents" — a native macOS app built on Ghostty with features like vertical tabs, notification rings, in-app browser, split panes, and a scriptable API.
